title: Tamil Nadu Assembly session LIVE: CM Vijay moves resolution seeking confidence vote
content: AIADMK MLAs from the faction supporting Edappadi K. Palaniswami have announced their intentions to vote against the confidence motion

title: Jammu and Kashmir leaders ask Omar Abdullah government to follow steps of Tamil Nadu CM Vijay on liquor ban
content: To make a point on how it was not an easy move to ban liquor in J&K, Mr. Abdullah referred to former Prime Minister Morarji Desai’s visit to J&K and his advocacy to ban liquor in J&K in the 1970s

title: Prateek Yadav, Mulayam Singh Yadav’s younger son, passes away in Lucknow
content: Parteek Yadav was rushed to Civil Hospital in Lucknow, where doctors declared him brought dead; the cause of death is yet to be ascertained

title: KSRTC staff unions to launch indefinite strike in Karnataka on May 20
content: Aim is to intensify pressure on the State Government over long-pending demands related to wages, arrears, employee welfare measures, and administrative issues

title: DC directs officials to ensure uninterrupted drinking water supply in Kalaburagi district of Karnataka
content: Zilla Panchayat Chief Executive Officer Bhanwar Singh Meena told PDOs to act responsibly in handling drinking water issues in rural areas

title: Chinese claims of ‘on site support’ to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor ‘corroborates’ what was known earlier: MEA
content: “Nations must reflect on whether supporting terror infrastructure affects their reputation and standing,” warns MEA; engineer confirms role of Chinese personnel in last year’s India-Pakistan clash

title: Trinamool’s land policy delayed West Bengal border fencing: MHA’s 2023 affidavit in Supreme Court
content: On December 11, 2023, the Ministry of Home Affairs informed the Supreme Court that the West Bengal government had not adopted the LARR Act 2013, which allows land acquisition for public purposes

title: Education Ministry gives fresh push for implementation of PM-SHRI in Bengal, Kerala, and T.N. post elections
content: Launched in September 2022, this five-year initiative aims to transform over 14,500 schools into “exemplar institutions” that lead the way in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020; only a limited time is left for the scheme to complete its five-year duration, the Ministry stated in a letter issued to the States

title: Elderly auto driver killed after e-scooter explosion sparks fire in house in Bengaluru
content: The family had plugged in their electric two-wheeler for charging around 11 p.m. before going to sleep. Around 3 a.m., the vehicle allegedly exploded, triggering a fire

title: ‘Don’t want to be part of biased exercise’: Rahul Gandhi records dissent on CBI director’s selection
content: LoP Rahul Gandhi said that despite repeated written requests, he was not provided with the self-appraisal reports or 360-degree reports of the eligible candidates.

title: IMD unveils weather model to provide ‘block level’ forecast of monsoon journey
content: It has been a long-standing aim of the IMD to provide hyper local forecasts to enable farmers to time their sowing precisely

title: We will not allow Tamil Thai Vaazhthu to be pushed behind during Governor’s Address, says  Udhayanidhi Stalin
content: The rendition of Tamil Thai Vaazhthu after the national song  Vande Mataram and the national anthem during the swearing-in of Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay had shocked the people of Tamil Nadu, says the Leader of the Opposition

title: Nitish Kumar directs JD(U) quota ministers to hold ‘Jan-Sunwai’ at party office five days a week
content: On the first day, Mr. Kumar also visited the party office to oversee the arrangements. He took stock of the proceedings and also interacted with the party workers, ministers and the general public present, inquiring about their well-being

title: Demographic shifts and the rise of Hindutva in Kanniyakumari district
content: Kanniyakumari holds a distinctive place in Tamil Nadu’s political history. Despite nearly six decades of Dravidian dominance in the State, it became the first district to elect a Hindutva-backed candidate as early as 1989, when V. Balachandram of Hindu Desam won the Padmanabhapuram constituency

title: Hanuman Jayanti: Muslim residents offer buttermilk to Hindu devotees in Kadapa days after clashes
content: The Collector and SP held negotiations between the two communities and increased police deployment for the procession

title: Government raises gold and silver tariffs to 15% to curb imports, support rupee
content: The higher duties could dampen demand in the world’s second-largest consumer of precious metals, although they may help narrow India’s trade ‌deficit and support the rupee, one of Asia’s worst-performing currencies

title: Watch: From MGR to EPS: The long history of revolts in Dravidian politics
content: With discomfort in the AIADMK showing no signs of subsiding, after former School Education Minister and veteran leader K.A. Sengottaiyan was divested of party posts following his call for the return of estranged leaders, it is a timely moment to revisit some of the most significant uprisings within Tamil Nadu’s two major Dravidian parties.

title: Why no FIR by Rajasthan SOG in NEET paper leak case, asks Congress
content: The Congress leaders said here that the Bharatiya Janata Party government had tried to suppress the matter for several days instead of finding out the truth

title: Mahatma Gandhi, his Salem visit and philately
content: The philatelic museum, which was set up in January 1997, exhibits commemorative stamps and first day postal covers issued by India and a host of countries including those in African and Caribbean regions on Gandhiji. It also showcases a reclining teakwood chair used by Gandhiji during his stay in Salem.

title: Watch: DMK MLA and LoP Udhayanidhi Stalin’s maiden speech in T.N. assembly
content: Leader of Opposition and DMK MLA Udhayanidhi Stalin addressed the Tamil Nadu Assembly on Tuesday and revived the anti-Sanatan pitch. He also reiterated call for its eradication

title: U.P. BJP announces State-wide protest over Samajwadi Party MP’s comment against PM Modi
content: U.P. CM Yogi Adityanath described the comment as an insult to the trust of 145 crore countrymen and the democratic dignity of India

title: Dharmendra Pradhan’s mind in Odisha while his body in Delhi, BJD charges over NEET paper leak
content: If Mr. Pradhan is unable to fulfil his ministerial responsibilities, he should immediately resign on moral grounds, the regional party demands

title: FIR filed after videos show Bajrang Dal workers assaulting man over ‘love jihad’
content: Bhopal Additional Commissioner of Police, Crime, Monika Shukla told The Hindu that the police have launched an investigation into the case and that the accused were yet to be identified

title: J&K L-G Manoj Sinha hardens stand on drug peddlers, days after Minister opposes demolitions of properties in Kashmir
content: With Mr. Sinha hardening his stand, the J&K police on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) stepped up actions against the accused in Kashmir

title: Diplomacy and sustainability- dialogues for the future
content: The Hindu Group, in association with Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences (SIMATS), will host The Hindu Diplomacy & Sustainability Dialogues 2026 on May 13 at SIMATS, Chennai

title: BJP targets DMK leader Udhayanidhi Stalin over Sanatana Dharma remarks
content: BJP spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla also targeted the Congress for “supporting” the anti-Hindu comment and questioned the “silence” of newly elected Chief Minister Vijay on the issue

title: Ever since Elon Musk took over, banning social media accounts became common: Iltija Mufti
content: She said banning the social media accounts has impacted her ability “to raise issues of public importance and reach out to people over misgovernance”

title: AICC begins consultations with Kerala leaders in New Delhi to decide on Congress’ CM candidate
content: The high command, according to a party insider, is reportedly seeking bipartisan support of top leaders, including former KPCC presidents, to restore the period of organisational and alliance stability, which delivered the Congress the momentous win

title: MGNREGS workers togo on strike on May 15
content: NREGA Sangharsh Morcha and All India Kisan Sabha have called for the strike against the government’s decision to discontinue the scheme.

title: ‘Bangla Pokkho’ founder Garga Chatterjee arrested for spreading EVM misinformation
content: Bangla Pokkho is a pro-Bengali advocacy organisation, and Mr. Chatterjee is often found campaigning for prioritising the Bengali language and Bengalis over other languages and communities in West Bengal

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled highlights: CBI takes custody of man detained by Nashik Police
content: The schedule for the re-examination would be announced within the “next seven to 10 days”, according to the DG of NTA, Abhishek Singh

title: Tamil Nadu’s political transition, growth story faultlines
content: The State’s development model is under pressure from changing realities, which the new Chief Minister needs to address

title: Baltimore ship crash: U.S. slaps criminal cases against Singapore, Chennai firms and Indian national
content: They have been charged with conspiracy, wilfully failing to immediately inform U.S. Coast Guard of a known hazardous condition, obstruction of an agency proceeding, and false statements

title: A society at large under question: rebuild a culture where safety of children is collective responsibility
content: The sexual assault and murder of a minor girl in Maharashtra has once again exposed the disturbing vulnerabilities that continue to confront children in our society

title: Floor test will be smooth; have received letters from EPS, Velumani factions: T.N. Assembly Speaker
content: “It is by the grace of God that Mr. Vijay has won. Our leader is going to prove his majority on May 13,” says Prabhakar, who exudes confidence that the young first-time MLAs will exceed expectations

title: West Godavari district of A.P. bets big on green silica industry
content: The paddy-rich district aims to process husk into precipitated silica and silica gel, which have high demand in automotive, cosmetric and other industries, ensuring monetisation of farm waste

title: GCC expedites work on various civic projects
content: Projects include restoration of Thamaraikulam in Ambattur zone and the urban primary health centre in Tondiarpet zone

title: TN govt. issues instructions regarding preferential reservation for SC (A) candidates
content: TN Arunthathiyars Act provides for 16% preferential reservation for Arunthathiyars within the overall 18% reservation for SC in admissions to educational institutions and in appointments/posts under the State

title: Quantum jump in followers of T.N. CMO’s social media handles after Vijay’s swearing-in
content: The number of followers on Instagram soared from four lakh to 2.1 million after C. Joseph Vijay took over as the Chief Minister

title: Elephant dies after fall near Nadugani ghat road
content: The elephant is suspected to have slipped from a cliff nearly 30 metres high before crashing onto the concrete structure below, say forest officials

title: Puducherry Pollution Control Committee launches e-waste inventorisation survey across U.T.
content: This initiative, in collaboration with the National Productivity Council, aims to compile a comprehensive inventory of bulk consumers, quantify e-waste generation, map sources, repair and dismantling units and evaluate current handling practices

title: Akhilesh Yadav's stepbrother, husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, Prateek dies at 38
content: Prateek Yadav, 38, stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, was found dead at Lucknow's Civil Hospital early Wednesday. Doctors declared him brought dead. The younger son of the late Mulayam Singh Yadav, Prateek was not involved in politics. A post-mortem is underway to determine the cause of his demise.

title: Congress delays, netizens decide: Kerala CM race sparks online carnival
content: Kerala's social media is buzzing with memes and online polls as the Congress leadership deliberates on the next Chief Minister.  V.D. Satheesan, who led the UDF campaign, is widely favored online, with many users urging a swift decision.  Ramesh Chennithala and K.C. Venugopal are also in contention, but public sentiment, expressed creatively online, leans heavily towards Satheesan.

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: GT surge to 99.6%, where do other teams stand?
content: With 14 games left, Gujarat Titans are almost guaranteed a top-four finish with a 99.6% chance, while RCB also has a strong 88.1% probability. Sunrisers Hyderabad and Punjab Kings' playoff hopes are still alive but diminished. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold a slight edge for the top four, with Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals facing slim chances.

title: Why Madras high court barred TVK MLA who won by 1 vote from Vijay’s floor test
content: Madras High Court has barred TVK MLA Srinivasa Sethupathi, who won by a single vote in Tirupattur, from participating in the upcoming floor test. The court cited the potential impact of his disputed vote on the state's constitutional governance. The MLA has appealed the order to the Supreme Court.

title: Trump administration sees 'no sign' of AI taking away jobs
content: The Trump administration is downplaying AI's impact on job losses, despite tech companies citing AI-driven restructuring for recent layoffs. While White House officials claim no current data shows AI costing jobs, major firms like Amazon and Meta are openly linking AI advancements to reduced workforce needs and increased productivity.

title: Saudi Arabia launched secret retaliatory airstrikes on Iran during regional war: Report
content: Saudi Arabia conducted unpublicized airstrikes on Iran in late March, marking the first direct military action on Iranian soil in retaliation for attacks within the kingdom. This shift in approach followed Iranian strikes on Gulf Cooperation Council states, leading to an informal de-escalation understanding between Riyadh and Tehran.

title: US imposes visa ban on Indians linked to alleged fentanyl trafficking network
content: The US has imposed visa restrictions on 13 individuals, including the owner of an Indian online pharmacy, KS International Traders, accused of selling illicit fentanyl-laced drugs to Americans. This action follows previous sanctions against Indian nationals and firms involved in trafficking fentanyl and its precursor chemicals, highlighting a joint effort between the US and India to combat drug networks.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Gill’s viral ‘dil abhi bhi sache’ post leaves fans in splits after win over SRH
content: Shubman Gill went viral after Gujarat Titans beat Sunrisers Hyderabad by 82 runs in IPL 2026. Gill posted “Dil abhi bhi sache, aur ghar bhi kache” on Instagram, referencing an old joke about SRH’s poor record against GT. On the field, half-centuries from Sai Sudharsan and Washington Sundar helped Gujarat dominate Hyderabad in Ahmedabad.

title: ‘Bad soul, going to divorce her’: Old post on wife surfaces after Mulayam's son's death
content: Prateek Yadav, stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, passed away in Lucknow. He was 38. Doctors declared him brought dead at Civil Hospital. The cause of death is under investigation. Prateek Yadav had recently accused his wife of damaging family relationships and planned a divorce.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Asian stocks today: Markets mixed as oil prices, inflation worries weigh on sentiment
content: Asian markets showed mixed trading as AI stock momentum waned, oil prices stayed high, and inflation worries persisted. Japan's Nikkei saw a slight gain, while South Korea's Kospi recovered some losses. Concerns over stalled US-Iran talks and hotter US inflation data contributed to investor caution across the region.

title: Will MS Dhoni likely travel with CSK squad for must-win game vs LSG?
content: MS Dhoni is reportedly traveling with the Chennai Super Kings squad to Lucknow for their IPL 2026 match against the Super Giants on May 15, despite being sidelined with injury. CSK, currently fifth in the standings, are on a strong comeback trail and aiming for a playoff berth, with Dhoni's potential return fueling anticipation.

title: Hantavirus outbreak: French woman critically ill, placed on artificial lung support
content: A French woman is in critical condition with hantavirus from a cruise ship outbreak. She is being treated with an artificial lung in Paris. The outbreak has caused multiple infections and deaths. Investigations are underway to find the source of the virus. The cruise ship is returning to the Netherlands for cleaning.

title: Hardik Pandya's social media post goes viral amid availability doubts - Watch
content: Hardik Pandya hinted at his recovery through an Instagram story showing him training at Mumbai’s Reliance facility after missing MI’s last game with back spasms. However, the Mumbai Indians skipper did not travel with the initial squad to Dharamsala for the Punjab Kings clash. MI coach Mahela Jayawardene confirmed Pandya remains under medical observation.

title: Gurgaon shocker: Cousin strangles woman to death, hides body in bed box
content: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly strangled to death by her maternal cousin in IMT Manesar, Gurgaon. The accused, who was seeking work, reportedly hid her body in a bed box. The murder was discovered by a colleague when the victim failed to report for work.

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares has chosen Narayana Hrudayalaya, Coal India, and Hyundai Motor India as the top stocks for buying today, May 13, 2026:

title: 'When I die, my money's not gonna come with me': Quote of the day by Heath Ledger
content: Late actor Heath Ledger, celebrated for his iconic Joker role, once shared profound thoughts on fame and fortune, emphasizing that wealth wouldn't accompany him in death. He believed his films would endure, reflecting his person. Ledger, who tragically passed away at 28 from an accidental overdose, consistently chose challenging roles, leaving a lasting cinematic legacy.

title: NEET paper sold at Rs 10L-25L: Tip to Rajasthan officials ignored, 'whistleblower' emailed NTA
content: The CBI has taken over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak investigation after Rajasthan Police revealed a "paper leak mafia" sold leaked exam questions disguised as a guess paper for up to Rs 25 lakh. The network spanned multiple states, with a suspect detained in Nashik. The leak's origin is believed to be outside Rajasthan.

title: Gold, silver import duty raised to 15% as govt moves to curb imports, support rupee
content: India has significantly increased import tariffs on gold and silver to 15% from 6% to curb imports and protect foreign exchange reserves. This move follows Prime Minister Modi's appeal for citizens to avoid gold purchases for a year, aiming to narrow the trade deficit and support the rupee amid global economic pressures.

title: Oil prices today: Crude slips after three-day rally as markets track Iran ceasefire
content: Oil prices dipped Wednesday after a three-day rally, with traders watching the Iran conflict's ceasefire and the Trump-Xi summit.  Despite a slight fall, crude remains above $100 a barrel due to ongoing supply fears.  Analysts predict elevated prices for months, impacting global inflation and potentially delaying US interest rate cuts.

title: PM Modi tells SPG to cut convoy by half, govt depts to adopt austerity measures
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has significantly reduced his motorcade size, aiming to inspire austerity across the government. The Special Protection Group is implementing this directive, cutting convoy vehicles by half while maintaining security standards. This move, coupled with the PM's call for reduced fuel and gold consumption, signals a broader push for cost-saving measures within ministries and departments.

title: Trump accuses US media of ‘giving Iran false hope’, calls coverage ‘virtual treason’
content: Former President Donald Trump accused American media of "virtual treason" for reporting that Iran inflicted damage on US forces, claiming such coverage gives Tehran "false hope." He asserted that Iran's military infrastructure has been destroyed, with "every single ship" sunk and its air force gone. Trump's remarks follow reports suggesting Iranian strikes caused significant damage to US military facilities.

title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47
content: Jason Collins, the NBA’s first openly gay active player, died at 47 after battling glioblastoma, a severe form of brain cancer. Collins built a respected 13-year NBA career and later became one of the most influential figures in modern sports after publicly coming out in 2013. Tributes from the NBA, former teams, and basketball leaders highlighted not only his courage and impact on inclusion but also his kindness away from the court.

title: ‘Rizwan’s reputation is really bad’: Litton Das’ brutal stump mic troll - Watch
content: Bangladesh secured a commanding 104-run victory over Pakistan in the first Test in Dhaka. A late collapse saw Pakistan lose seven wickets in the final session. Nahid Rana's exceptional bowling performance was key to Bangladesh's win. This marks Pakistan's first-ever Test defeat in Bangladesh. The series continues with the second Test in Sylhet.

title: Hegseth pushes Trump’s $1.5 trillion defense budget for 2027
content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defended President Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion military budget amid congressional scrutiny over the Iran war's escalating costs, now at $29 billion. Lawmakers questioned the strain on weapons stockpiles, while Hegseth acknowledged increased production to support ongoing operations and future needs.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' nears Rs 1,800 crore mark ahead of OTT debut
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its impressive box office streak, nearing Rs 1,800 crore globally ahead of its OTT debut. The action film, still drawing crowds after two months, has amassed over Rs 1,143 crore in India net and Rs 426 crore overseas. Audiences can anticipate an extended version on Netflix and JioHotstar soon.

title: New shot unlocked! Sundar's outrageous six shocks everyone - watch
content: Washington Sundar's astonishing, near-ground six was a highlight as Gujarat Titans defeated Sunrisers Hyderabad by 82 runs. Sundar's 50, alongside Sai Sudharsan's 61, propelled GT to 168. Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder then starred with the ball, bowling out SRH for just 86 to secure a dominant victory.

title: Alia Bhatt meets Carlos Sainz at Cannes; Bollywood & F1 crossover delights fans - watch
content: Bollywood star Alia Bhatt and Formula One racer Carlos Sainz Jr. created a stir at the 2026 Cannes Film Festival. Viral photos captured the duo engaging in conversation and posing together, sparking widespread fan excitement and social media buzz.

title: EAM’s bilateral with Iran’s Araghchi on Friday, energy disruption & connectivity on agenda
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar will host his Iranian counterpart for bilateral talks focusing on energy and connectivity concerns, including stranded LPG tankers and Chabahar port operations. This meeting, occurring during the BRICS foreign ministers' gathering, aims to address regional conflicts and foster cooperation among member states.

title: From surviving gang-ravaged streets to pinnacle of NBA: Isiah Thomas way
content: In this exclusive interview with TimesofIndia.com, Isiah Thomas reflects on his journey from Chicago’s gang-ravaged West Side to NBA greatness. The Detroit Pistons legend spoke about survival instincts, leadership, the “mental over physical” philosophy behind the Bad Boys era, business lessons after basketball, his long-held vision of positionless basketball, and more.

title: 'Oil cos losing Rs 1,000 crore a day': Hike in prices imminent, hints Petroleum minister Puri
content: Public sector oil companies are facing daily losses of Rs 1,000 crore, potentially forcing fuel price hikes soon. Minister Hardeep Singh Puri highlighted significant under-recoveries, warning that companies cannot sustain these losses indefinitely. While elections are not the reason for the freeze, a drop in crude prices or reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could offer relief.

title: CBI takes over Suvendu ex-aide murder probe
content: The CBI has filed an FIR and initiated an investigation into the murder of Chandranath Rath, former personal assistant to West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari. A seven-member SIT, led by a DIG-rank officer, has been formed to probe the case, with a meeting held at CBI headquarters to review developments and seek remand of arrested suspects.

title: Monsoon may hit Andamans this week: IMD
content: The India Meteorological Department has launched an AI-enabled monsoon forecasting system offering localized weather information up to four weeks in advance for farmers. A pilot service for Uttar Pradesh provides high-resolution rainfall forecasts at 1km resolution for up to 10 days, utilizing advanced AI and observational data.

title: Even Mahabharat would pale: Supreme Court on Kapur spat
content: A protracted property dispute between Sunjay Kapur's mother and wife has prompted the Supreme Court to appoint former CJI D Y Chandrachud as mediator. The apex court noted the legal battle's intensity, comparing it to the Mahabharat. A fresh plea alleges circumvention of a previous order, with the court set to hear the matter further.

title: Gabrielle Union opens up on menopause struggles and fading self-worth
content: Dwyane Wade and his wife, Gabrielle Union, might be one of the most loved couples in the world but they continue to maintain privacy as they try to balance their busy professional and personal lives as they remain private. While Dwyane Wade has advocated for men’s health over the past year, Gabrielle Union has also not shied away from speaking about the issues she has experienced.

title: PM Modi Orders Cut In Convoy Size, Calls For More EVs In Austerity Push
content: Other ministers, including Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta and Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav, have decided to reduce the number of vehicles in their official convoys.

title: Prateek Yadav Death News LIVE: Prateek Yadav, Mulayam Singh Yadav's Son, Dies At 38
content: Prateek Yadav Death Updates: Prateek Yadav was taken to Lucknow's Civil Hospital, where he was declared brought dead.

title: Explained: Why India Raised Gold Import Duty, And What It Means For You
content: India's forex reserves have slipped amid the Iran war. By raising gold import duty, India is taking pre-emptive steps to save its forex reserves.

title: Centre Raises Gold, Silver Import Tariffs To 15% Amid PM's Forex Warning
content: The government has imposed a 10% basic customs duty and a 5% Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess (AIDC) on gold and silver imports, taking the effective import tax to 15% from 6%.

title: Trump China Visit Live Updates: Trump Heads To China For High-Stakes Talks With Xi Over Iran War, Trade
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: Vijay Hopes To Pass Confidence Vote, Fractured AIADMK To Support
content: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: A rebel faction of the AIADMK, comprising 30 MLAs, said they will support Vijay in Wednesday's floor test.

title: Boy Beaten To Death For Plucking Lemons From Tree In Bihar: Cops
content: According to villagers, a dispute erupted after the boy plucked lemons from the tree of the accused. The incident occurred at Sakrawa village in Bihar's Nalanda.

title: Exclusive: Satellite Image Confirms Pak May Be Shielding Iran's Military Aircraft
content: Iran sent multiple aircraft including a reconnaissance and intelligence plane to Pakistan's Nur Khan airbase soon after US President Donald Trump announced the ceasefire in April, CBS News reported

title: Born A Month Ago, 4 Cheetah Cubs Found Dead In Kuno National Park
content: Four cheetah cubs born in April were found dead in Kuno National Park with partially eaten carcasses.

title: After PM Modi's Appeal, Delhi Chief Minister Curbs Official Vehicles
content: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta said that she herself, her Cabinet colleagues, BJP MLAs, public representatives, officers and Delhi government departments would minimise the use of official vehicles.

title: Trump Heads To China For High-Stakes Talks With Xi On Tehran, Taiwan And Trade
content: When the leaders of the globe's two economic powerhouses meet, they are set to tackle a loaded agenda, from US arms transfers to Taiwan, Beijing's rare earth export restrictions, the situation in Iran, to the state of bilateral trade relations.

title: "Going To Do Whatever Is Necessary": Trump On Russian Oil Waiver
content: The United States had eased sanctions on Russian oil in mid-March to counteract global oil shortages resulting from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Which Way Will AIADMK Rebels Turn? Trust Vote For Vijay Is EPS Battleground
content: The majority mark in the Tamil Nadu assembly is 118 and Vijay's TVK currently has 107 MLAs, as Vijay resigned one of the two seats he contested from.

title: How A Hostel Owner In Rajasthan's Sikar Helped Unravel NEET Paper Leak
content: The "guess paper" had an amazing strike rate -- 135 questions had matched originals. The hostel owner and the chemistry teacher then decided to approach the local police.

title: NEET Paper Reached Rajasthan From Nashik Via Haryana, Reveals Probe
content: NEET Paper Leak: According to police, the PDF had two sets of Chemistry and Biology. So, 45 questions of Chemistry and 90 questions of Biology were found to be the perfect match along with their answers.

title: Trump 'Golden Dome' Missile Shield Could Cost $1.2 Trillion Over 20 Years
content: In May 2025, Trump announced that $25 billion had been earmarked for the project, estimating its total cost at $175 billion.

title: Trump Posts Graphic Of Venezuela As 51st US State
content: Trump posted a map graphic on his Truth Social platform depicting Venezuela with an inset American flag and the label "51st State."

title: NEET-UG exam cancelled after 'paper leak', 23 lakh students hit, CBI to probe case
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

content: the report of Pakistan giving sanctuary to Iranian military aircraft revives one of the subcontinent's great geopolitical ironies . the claims were implausible because the Nur Khan base sits "right in the heart of the city" the US administration has stopped short of publicly accusing Islamabad of wrongdoing .
